title: Run-length encoding is lossless: decode ∘ encode = id
statement_informal: RLE collapses maximal runs of equal elements into (value,count) pairs; decoding expands them with repeat. The round-trip law decode (encode l) = l is the correctness statement every lossless compressor must satisfy.

cost_model.notes: encode is one pass merging adjacent equal elements; decode expands via repeat. Round-trip proved by induction on the list, casing on the head of encode xs.

references: Run-length encoding — a classic lossless compression primitive; correctness = decode ∘ encode = id.
